senses.definition: used to refer to something that is free of any moisture

examples.x: After days of scorching heat, the ground became dry as a bone, cracking underfoot.
The desert landscape stretched for miles, with the sand dunes appearing dry as a bone under the blazing sun.
The drought had taken its toll on the once-lush farmland, leaving it dry as a bone and causing crop failures.
The towel had been left out in the sun for hours, and now it was dry as a bone, ready for use.
Despite the heavy rain shower, the porch remained dry as a bone, shielded from the downpour by the awning.

word_list.pronunciation: sˈoʊkt wˈɛt dɹˈɛntʃt tə ðə skˈɪn

senses.definition: completely covered with water or some other liquid

examples.x: After walking home in the pouring rain, she was soaked to the skin, with her clothes clinging damply to her body.
The swimmer emerged from the pool, drenched to the skin, water dripping from every part of her body.
The sudden downpour caught them off guard, leaving them wet to the skin within minutes, their clothes completely soaked.
Despite wearing a raincoat, the hiker got caught in a storm and ended up drenched to the skin, his boots squelching with every step.
They decided to have a water fight in the backyard, and within minutes, everyone was soaked to the skin, laughing and shivering in the cool water.

senses.definition: used to refer to something that has a very considerable or unusual length

examples.x: The grocery list was long as my arm, filled with items I needed to buy for the week.
She had a to-do list as long as her arm, filled with chores and errands that needed to be completed.
The line at the amusement park was long as my arm, stretching far beyond what I expected.
